text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy little village of Willowbrook, there was an old owl named Owly. Owly wasn;.you might believe she existed. You see, Owly spent most of her life high in the oak trees overlooking Plumstead Fields.

But one foggy morning, Owly heard whispers. They didn';t say much. just fluttered colder. Owl feathered wings!. oh yes.

Who knew?

Owly flew down. Little birds told tales. They saw something odd. Big cats!

They were fierce!

Owly watched. It was strange. They fed.

Not food.

A catnip flute.

They liked that.

Then one stole it.

No no no!

Quickly Owl!.

They ran!. Owly!

They disappeared.

Now don';t cry.

She flew higher.

That;s better.

Sometimes you just have to leap.

Owly soared.

That;s fun!.

Sometimes you think.

Maybe I'll hop!

Here goes!

Whoow!
